limitation: This is an interested belligerent's official claim and names neither vessel; it does not independently establish warning receipt, navigation-system status, route condition, U.S. direction, strike attribution, or disablement.
observation: In Persian Operation Nasr 2 Notice No. 6, IRGC Public Relations claimed that two supertankers ignored repeated Strait of Hormuz security-control warnings, switched off navigation systems, attempted a mined route after U.S. encouragement, and were struck and disabled.
